title: VeritasChain Protocol (VCP) Event - Version 1.2
description: JSON Schema for VCP v1.2 events in the structured Header/Payload/Security encoding. This is the encoding used when a VCP Event is carried as the payload of a SCITT Signed Statement (draft-kamimura-scitt-vcp) and in VCP v1.1+ implementation examples. This schema enforces protocol-level requirements only. Conformance-tier and VC-Certified requirements (for example, mandatory MerkleRoot, MerkleIndex, and AnchorReference under v1.1+ certification, per Specification Section 6.4.1) are Registration Policy and certification matters and are intentionally not enforced here.
$comment: Maintainer: VeritasChain Standards Organization (VSO). Source specification: https://github.com/veritaschain/vcp-spec/tree/main/spec/v1.2 . License: CC BY 4.0.

UUID.$comment: UUIDv7 (RFC 9562) is required for time-sortable EventIDs at Gold/Platinum tiers; the pattern accepts any RFC 9562 UUID.

DecimalString.$comment: Specification Section 4.2.2: all financial numeric values MUST be encoded as strings to avoid IEEE 754 precision loss under RFC 8785 canonicalization.

EventID.description: Unique event identifier. UUIDv7 (RFC 9562) provides time-sortable identification.

TraceID.description: OPTIONAL transaction trace identifier (for example, CAT Rule 613 correlation).

TimestampISO.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp, UTC, up to nanosecond precision.

TimestampInt.description: Nanoseconds since the Unix epoch (UTC), as a 64-bit integer.
TimestampInt.$comment: Values exceed 2^53; parsers must preserve 64-bit integer precision.

EventType.description: VCP v1.2 Event Type Registry: core types (v1.0), standardized error types (v1.1), and ERASURE (v1.2).

ActorID.description: Identifier of the entity generating this event.

ChainID.description: Identifier of the per-Actor hash chain.

SequenceNum.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number within the Actor's chain.

PolicyID.description: Registration Policy identifier. SHOULD follow the Issuer Domain + Local ID convention (reverse_domain:local_identifier), for example 'org.veritaschain.policy:gold-v1-2' (Specification Section 5.5.4).

SCITTAlignment.description: OPTIONAL SCITT alignment object (v1.2). REQUIRED under the VCP_SCITT conformance profile; RECOMMENDED under VCP_REGULATORY.

Version.description: VCP specification version governing the alignment semantics.

StatementID.description: Identifier of the SCITT Signed Statement carrying this event.

TransparencyServiceID.description: Identifier of the Transparency Service with which the event is registered. REQUIRED under the VCP_SCITT profile.

Payload.description: Domain data organized into VCP-* modules. Module names MUST match ^VCP-[A-Z]+$.

VCP-TRADE.description: Trading data: orders, executions, modifications, cancellations.

VCP-RISK.description: Risk management data: exposure, limits, margin.

VCP-GOV.description: Governance data: algorithm parameters, decision factors, operator actions.

DecisionFactors.description: Compact form: array of factor names. Extended form: object per Specification Section 5.1.1.

ConfidenceScore.description: Confidence in [0.0, 1.0]. String encoding is RECOMMENDED for precision (Specification Section 5.1.1).

VCP-PRIVACY.description: Privacy metadata: encrypted-field inventory, key references, retention policy, and (v1.2) ERASURE event fields.

ErasureTargetEventIDs.description: Events whose protected fields are crypto-shredded (ERASURE events).

KeyDestructionProof.description: Evidence that the DEK was destroyed (for example, an HSM or KMS attestation).

RetentionExemption.description: Legal basis where a retention obligation overrides erasure (for example, MiFID II Article 16(7)).

VCP-XREF.description: Cross-reference data, including v1.2 multi-actor chain linking.

VCP-RECOVERY.description: Recovery operations, subject to the v1.2 operational constraints.

Security.$comment: VC-Certified v1.1+ additionally requires MerkleRoot, MerkleIndex, and AnchorReference (Specification Section 6.4.1). Those are certification requirements enforced by Registration Policy, not by this protocol-level schema. In the SCITT Signed Statement encoding, the event signature MAY be carried by the COSE_Sign1 envelope, in which case the Signature field is omitted here.

EventHash.description: Hash of the canonicalized (RFC 8785) Header and Payload. Production values use the algorithm-prefixed form 'sha256:' followed by 64 lowercase hex characters.

PrevHash.description: OPTIONAL hash of the previous event in this Actor's chain; absent for INIT events.

Signature.description: Base64-encoded signature over the event. MAY be omitted in the SCITT encoding, where COSE_Sign1 carries the signature.

SignAlgo.description: Signature algorithm registry value. ED25519 is the default. DILITHIUM2 (ML-DSA, FIPS 204) and FALCON512 hold EXPERIMENTAL status in v1.2. RSA_2048 is DEPRECATED.

PQCSignature.description: OPTIONAL post-quantum signature for dual-signature deployments (Specification Appendix E).

AnchorRef.description: Reference to the external anchor location (draft-kamimura-scitt-vcp field name).

AnchorReference.description: Reference to the anchor record (Specification Section 6.4 field name).

PolicyIdentification.description: OPTIONAL top-level Policy Identification block (Specification Section 5.5).
